input: This Key Facts Statement (KFS) is provided to you before your purchase of the ABC Global Balanced Fund. The fund invests in a diversified mix of equities and fixed income instruments across global markets. The ongoing charges figure is 1.25% per annum, which covers the management fee and other expenses but excludes transaction costs. Past performance is not indicative of future results. You should read the full Explanatory Memorandum before investing. If you have any questions, please contact your SFC-licensed distributor.

As part of our licensed corporation's ongoing obligations under the Securities and Futures Ordinance, we are required to keep proper records of all client transactions and communications for a minimum period of seven years. These records must be readily accessible for inspection by the SFC upon request. We also conduct annual internal audits to ensure our record-keeping practices remain compliant, and our Responsible Officer reviews audit findings and reports material deficiencies to the board within 30 days of completion.

As your appointed insurance agent, I am required to disclose that I am an agent of Prudential Hong Kong Limited and act solely in the capacity of that insurer's representative. This means my primary duty is to that insurer and not to you as a policyholder when it comes to product recommendation. Before recommending this critical illness plan, I have assessed your financial situation and insurance needs based on the information you provided. I encourage you to read the policy document carefully and ask questions about any terms you do not fully understand before signing.

We wish to inform you that the governing rules of your MPF scheme have been amended to reflect updated fee structures effective from 1 January next year. Under the Mandatory Provident Fund Schemes Ordinance, we are required to provide you with at least three months' advance notice of any change to scheme rules that may materially affect your interests. This notification constitutes that required notice. The revised fee schedule is enclosed with this letter. If you have questions, please contact your scheme administrator.

This bond is issued pursuant to a prospectus registered with the Companies Registry of Hong Kong. Investors should be aware that this instrument carries credit risk — the possibility that the issuer may default on interest payments or fail to repay principal at maturity. The bond is not principal-protected and is not equivalent to a bank deposit. Interest rate risk is also present: rising market interest rates will cause the bond's market price to decline before maturity. Please read the full prospectus, including the risk factors section, before making any investment decision.

Our AML risk assessment for the current period classified this customer as high-risk based on the following factors: the customer is a politically exposed person, the source of wealth involves jurisdictions on the FATF high-risk country list, and transactions in the past six months showed a pattern inconsistent with the stated business purpose. Accordingly, we have applied enhanced due diligence measures including senior management approval for the relationship, monthly transaction monitoring reviews, and a request for additional documentation on the source of funds for the three largest transactions.

Thank you for submitting your complaint regarding the delay in processing your redemption request. We acknowledge receipt of your complaint and confirm that it has been logged in our complaints register. Under our procedures, we aim to provide you with a substantive written response within 21 calendar days of acknowledgement. If our investigation requires more time, we will notify you of the delay and provide an expected resolution date. This complaint has been assigned to our Client Services team, and you will be contacted by a designated handler within two business days.

In accordance with our continuous disclosure obligations as a listed issuer under the Rules Governing the Listing of Securities on The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, we wish to notify shareholders that the expected profit for the financial year ended 31 December will be materially lower than market consensus. This deviation is attributable to a one-off impairment charge on our mainland China operations. We estimate the impairment at approximately HK$450 million. Further details will be provided in our annual results announcement. This announcement is made voluntarily in advance of our results to ensure fair and timely disclosure.

Our SFC Type 1 licence is due for renewal and we have submitted the required renewal application to the SFC well in advance of the expiry date. As part of the renewal, we have confirmed that our Responsible Officers continue to meet the competence requirements, including the completion of the required continuing professional training hours. Our financial resources have been verified to meet the applicable liquid capital ratio. We have also updated our disclosure of the names of all Responsible Officers on the SFC's public register as required.

Under the HKMA's Supervisory Policy Manual module IR-1, our institution has established a comprehensive Interest Rate Risk in the Banking Book framework. Our IRRBB measurement includes parallel shift scenarios of plus and minus 200 basis points, as required. The Economic Value of Equity sensitivity is stress-tested quarterly and reported to the Asset-Liability Management Committee. Our Net Interest Income sensitivity analysis covers a rolling 12-month horizon. Material IRRBB positions are escalated to the board on a semi-annual basis, and breaches of internal limits are reported to HKMA as part of our regular supervisory reporting.
